0.ai_notes: '2.': This is a numbering marker used to organize points in a list.
0.remarks: '2.': 列表中的序号标记，用于组织条目。

1.original: Practice calling people by their names.
1.b1: Practice using people's names when you speak to them.
1.cn: 练习在称呼别人时使用他们的名字。
1.ai_notes: 'Practice': to do something repeatedly to get better at it.
1.remarks: 'Practice': 练习，通过反复做某事来提高技能。

2.original: Every year, shrewd manufacturers sell more briefcases, pencils, Bibles, and hundreds of other items just by putting the buyer's name on the product.
2.b1: Every year, smart companies sell more products like briefcases and pencils by putting the customer's name on them.
2.cn: 每年，精明的制造商通过在公文包、铅笔、圣经等数百种产品上印上买家的名字，卖出了更多的商品。
2.ai_notes: 'Shrewd': clever and good at judging situations to gain an advantage. 'Briefcase': a flat, rectangular bag used for carrying documents.
2.remarks:
'Shrewd': 精明的，善于判断局势以获取利益。
'Briefcase': 公文包，用于携带文件的扁平长方形包。

3.original: People like to be called by name.
3.b1: People like it when you use their names.
3.ai_notes: 'Called by name': to be addressed using one's personal name rather than a title or nothing at all.
3.remarks: 'Called by name': 直呼其名，指用个人名字而非头衔或不称呼来称呼某人。

4.original: It gives everyone a boost to be addressed by name.
4.b1: It makes people feel good when you address them by name.
4.cn: 被人直呼其名会让人感到振奋。
4.ai_notes: 'Boost': something that helps improve or increase something. 'Addressed': to speak or write to someone.
4.remarks:
'Boost': 促进，帮助提升或增加某事物。
'Addressed': 称呼或向某人讲话/写信。

5.original: Two special things you must remember.
5.b1: There are two important things to remember.
5.cn: 有两件特别的事情你必须记住。
5.ai_notes: 'Special': something that is important or different from the usual.
5.remarks: 'Special': 特别的，指重要或与众不同的事物。

6.original: Pronounce the name correctly and spell it correctly.
6.b1: Say the name correctly and write it correctly.
6.cn: 发音要准确，拼写也要正确。
6.ai_notes: 'Pronounce': to make the sound of a word or name. 'Spell': to write or name the letters that form a word.
6.remarks:
'Pronounce': 发音，读出单词或名字的声音。
'Spell': 拼写，写出或读出构成单词的字母。

7.original: If you mispronounce or misspell someone's name, that person feels that you feel he is unimportant.
7.b1: If you say or spell a name wrong, the person will feel you do not think they are important.
7.cn: 如果你读错或拼错别人的名字，那个人会觉得你认为他不重要。
7.ai_notes: 'Mispronounce': to say a word or name incorrectly. 'Unimportant': not having much value or significance.
7.remarks:
'Mispronounce': 发音错误，读错单词或名字。
'Unimportant': 不重要的，价值或意义不大。

8.b1: Here is one more important tip.
8.cn: 这里还有一个特别的提醒。
8.ai_notes: 'Reminder': a note or message that helps you remember something.
8.remarks: 'Reminder': 提醒物，帮助你记起某事的便条或信息。

9.original: When talking with people you don't know well, add the appropriate title,
9.b1: When you talk to people you do not know well, use a title.
9.cn: 当与不太熟悉的人交谈时，加上适当的称谓。
9.ai_notes: 'Appropriate': suitable or right for a particular situation. 'Title': a word like Mr., Mrs., or Dr. used before a name.
9.remarks:
'Appropriate': 合适的，针对特定情况得体的。
'Title': 头衔，如Mr.、Mrs.或Dr.等放在名字前的称谓。

10.original: Miss, Mr. or Mrs. The office boy prefers Mr. Jones to just Jones.
10.b1: Use Miss, Mr. or Mrs. An office boy prefers to be called Mr. Jones instead of just Jones.
10.cn: 比如小姐、先生或太太。办公室的小伙子更喜欢被称为琼斯先生，而不是仅仅叫琼斯。
10.ai_notes: 'Office boy': a traditional term for a young male employee who does general tasks in an office.
10.remarks: 'Office boy': 勤杂工，传统上指在办公室做杂务的年轻男性员工。

11.b1: Your assistant feels the same way.
11.cn: 你的初级助理也是这样。
11.ai_notes: 'Junior assistant': a person who is at a lower level or has less experience in a job.
11.remarks: 'Junior assistant': 初级助理，职位较低或经验较少的人。

12.original: So do people at every level.
12.b1: People at every level feel the same.
12.cn: 各行各业的人都是如此。
12.ai_notes: 'At every level': referring to people in all positions, from the lowest to the highest in a group or company.
12.remarks: 'At every level': 各个层面，指组织中从低到高的所有职位。

13.original: These little titles help tremendously to make people feel important.
13.b1: These small titles help make people feel important.
13.cn: 这些小小的称谓对于让人们感到自己很重要非常有帮助。
13.ai_notes: 'Tremendously': to a very great degree or extent.

14.ai_notes: '3.': This is a numbering marker used to organize points in a list.
14.remarks: '3.': 列表中的序号标记，用于组织条目。

15.b1: Do not keep all the credit for yourself.
15.ai_notes: 'Hog': to take or keep all of something for yourself. 'Glory': praise and honor for an achievement.
15.remarks:
'Hog': 独占，将某物据为己有。
'Glory': 荣誉，因成就而获得的赞美。

16.ai_notes: 'Invest': to put time or effort into something to get a good result later.
16.remarks: 'Invest': 投入，为日后获得好结果而付出时间和精力。

17.original: Just recently I was a guest at an all-day sales convention.
17.b1: I recently attended a sales meeting that lasted all day.
17.cn: 最近我参加了一个全天的销售大会。
17.ai_notes: 'Convention': a large meeting of people who share the same interest or job.
17.remarks: 'Convention': 大会，具有相同兴趣或职业的人举行的大型会议。

18.original: After dinner that evening, the vice president in charge of sales for the company passed out awards to the two district managers,
18.b1: After dinner, the sales vice president gave awards to two district managers.
18.cn: 那天晚饭后，负责销售的副总裁给两位区域经理颁发了奖项。
18.ai_notes: 'Vice president': a high-ranking executive in a company. 'Passed out': to distribute items to a group of people.
18.remarks:
'Vice president': 副总裁，公司的高级管理人员。
'Passed out': 分发，将物品分给一群人。

19.original: a man and a woman, whose sales organizations had attained the best records for the year just ended.
19.b1: They were a man and a woman whose teams had the best sales results for the year.
19.cn: 他们是一男一女，他们的销售团队在刚过去的一年中取得了最好的成绩。
19.ai_notes: 'Sales organizations': groups of people working together to sell products. 'Attained': to succeed in achieving something.
19.remarks:
'Sales organizations': 销售组织，共同销售产品的群体。
'Attained': 达到，成功实现某目标。

20.original: Then the vice president asked those district managers to take fifteen minutes to tell the entire group how their organizations did so exceptionally well.
20.b1: The vice president asked them to explain to the group how their teams were so successful.
20.cn: 然后副总裁请这两位区域经理花十五分钟向全体人员讲述他们的团队是如何取得如此优异成绩的。

21.original: The first district manager, who I later learned had been appointed a manager only three months before and was therefore only partially responsible for his organization's record,
21.b1: The first manager had only been in the job for three months, so he was not fully responsible for the results.
21.cn: 第一位区域经理，我后来得知他三个月前才被任命为经理，因此他只对团队的成绩负有部分责任。
21.ai_notes: 'Appointed': to be chosen for a job or position. 'Partially': not completely; only in part.
21.remarks:
'Appointed': 被任命，被选定担任某职位。
'Partially': 部分地，不完全地。

22.original: got up and explained how he did it.
22.b1: He stood up and explained how he did it.
22.cn: 他站起来解释他是如何做到的。
22.ai_notes: 'Got up': stood up from a sitting position.
22.remarks: 'Got up': 起身，从坐着的状态站起来。

23.original: He conveyed the impression that his efforts, and his efforts alone, caused the sales increase.
23.b1: He acted as if only his own hard work caused the sales to increase.
23.cn: 他给人的印象是，销售额的增长完全归功于他个人的努力。
23.ai_notes: 'Conveyed the impression': to give people a specific idea or feeling. 'Alone': by himself, without help from others.
23.remarks:
'Conveyed the impression': 给人留下印象，传达某种感觉。
'Alone': 独自，没有他人帮助。

24.original: Remarks such as, When I took over, I did such and such.
24.b1: He said things like, 'When I started, I did this and that.'
24.cn: 他说了一些诸如“当我接手时，我做了这样那样的事”之类的话。
24.ai_notes: 'Remarks': words spoken or written about something.
24.remarks: 'Remarks': 言论，关于某事的口头或书面评价。

25.original: Things were in a mess, but I cleared them up.
25.b1: 'Things were a mess, but I fixed them.'
25.cn: “当时情况一团糟，但我把它们理顺了。”
25.ai_notes: 'In a mess': in a state of confusion or disorder. 'Cleared them up': to fix or solve problems.
25.remarks:
'In a mess': 一团糟，处于混乱状态。
'Cleared them up': 澄清/解决，修复或解决问题。

26.original: It wasn't easy, but I just grabbed hold of the situation and wouldn't let go, characterized his talk.
26.b1: 'It was hard, but I took control and did not give up.'
26.cn: “这并不容易，但我抓住了局面，没有放弃。”
26.ai_notes: 'Grabbed hold of': to take control of a situation. 'Characterized': to describe the typical quality of something.
26.remarks:
'Grabbed hold of': 掌控，控制住局面。
'Characterized': 描述特征，刻画某事物的典型品质。

27.original: As he talked, I could see the increasing resentment gathering in the faces of his salesmen.
27.b1: While he talked, I saw that his salespeople were becoming angry.
27.cn: 当他说话时，我能看到他的销售人员脸上逐渐聚集的怨气。
27.ai_notes: 'Resentment': a feeling of anger because you have been treated unfairly. 'Gathering': building up or increasing.
27.remarks:
'Resentment': 怨恨，因受到不公平对待而产生的愤怒。
'Gathering': 积聚，逐渐增加。

28.original: They were being ignored for the sake of the district manager's personal glory.
28.b1: He ignored them because he wanted all the glory for himself.
28.cn: 为了个人的荣耀，他完全忽视了他们。
28.ai_notes: 'Ignored': to pay no attention to someone. 'For the sake of': for the purpose of.
28.remarks:
'Ignored': 忽视，不理会某人。
'For the sake of': 为了……起见，为了某种目的。

29.original: Their hard work, which was responsible for the sales increase, was completely unrecognized.
29.b1: He did not give them any credit for their hard work.
29.cn: 他们的辛勤工作是销售额增长的原因，却完全没有得到认可。
29.ai_notes: 'Unrecognized': not given credit or notice for something done.
29.remarks: 'Unrecognized': 未被认可的，所做之事未获得赞赏或注意。

30.original: Then the second district manager got up to make her short talk, but this lady used an entirely different approach.
30.b1: Then the second manager spoke, and she had a much better way of doing it.
30.cn: 接着第二位区域经理站起来做简短发言，但这位女士采取了完全不同的方式。
30.ai_notes: 'Approach': a way of dealing with a situation or person.
30.remarks: 'Approach': 方法，处理某事或某人的方式。

31.original: First, she explained that the reason for her organization's success was the wholehearted effort of her sales force.
31.b1: First, she said her team's success was because of the hard work of all her salespeople.
31.cn: 首先，她解释说她团队成功的秘诀是全体销售人员全心全意的努力。
31.ai_notes: 'Wholehearted': done with complete sincerity and enthusiasm. 'Sales force': the group of people employed to sell a company's products.
31.remarks:
'Wholehearted': 全心全意的，真诚且充满热情的。
'Sales force': 销售团队，受雇销售公司产品的员工群体。

32.original: Then she asked each one to stand and paid a sincere personal compliment to each for his or her efforts.
32.b1: Then she asked everyone to stand and thanked them for their hard work.
32.cn: 然后她请每一个人站起来，并真诚地称赞了他们每个人的付出。
32.ai_notes: 'Sincere': honest and genuine. 'Compliment': a polite expression of praise.
32.remarks:
'Sincere': 真诚的，诚实且真实的。
'Compliment': 赞美，礼貌地表达夸奖。

33.ai_notes: 'Note': to pay attention to or observe something.

34.original: The first manager squandered the vice president's praise entirely on himself.
34.b1: The first manager took all the praise for himself.
34.cn: 第一位经理把副总裁的赞美全部揽到了自己身上。
34.ai_notes: 'Squandered': to use something in a wasteful way.

35.original: In doing so, he offended his own people.
35.b1: By doing this, he made his team unhappy.
35.cn: 这样做，他冒犯了自己的员工。
35.ai_notes: 'Offended': to make someone feel upset or insulted.
35.remarks: 'Offended': 被冒犯，感到难过或受辱。

36.cn: 他的销售团队士气低落。
36.remarks: 'Demoralized': 士气低落的，失去信心或希望的。

37.original: The second passed the praise on to her sales force, where it could do more good.
37.b1: The second manager shared the praise with her team, which was much better.
37.cn: 第二位经理把赞美传递给了她的销售团队，这更有意义。
37.ai_notes: 'Passed on': to give something to someone else.
37.remarks: 'Passed on': 传递，将某物交给他人。

38.original: This manager knew that praise, like money, can be invested to pay dividends.
38.b1: She knew that praise is like money; you can invest it to get better results.
38.cn: 这位经理知道，赞美就像金钱一样，可以投资以获得回报。
38.ai_notes: 'Dividends': a benefit or reward that you get from an investment.
38.remarks: 'Dividends': 红利，从投资中获得的利益或回报。

39.original: She knew that passing the credit on to her sales people would make them work even harder next year.
39.b1: She knew that giving credit to her team would make them work harder next year.
39.cn: 她知道，把功劳归于销售人员会让他们明年工作得更努力。
39.ai_notes: 'Passing the credit on': giving recognition to others for their work.
39.remarks: 'Passing the credit on': 把功劳归于他人，认可他人的工作成果。

40.b1: Remember, praise is a powerful tool.
40.ai_notes: 'Power': the ability to influence or change things.
40.remarks: 'Power': 权力，影响或改变事物的能力。
